zh.lifeSummary: 當Helen Smythe於1579年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾紐波特 帕格爾時，她的父親Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgate38歲，母親Anne Lynford38歲。她和John Ryder至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1677年在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾諾薩普頓享年98歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾諾薩普頓哥吉拉 沙也 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Helen Smythe于1579年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾紐波特 帕格爾时，她的父亲Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgate38岁，母亲Anne Lynford38岁。她和John Ryder至少有3个儿子和3个女儿。她去世于1677年在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾諾薩普頓享年98岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾諾薩普頓哥吉拉 沙也 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Smythe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Smith
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

nameMeanings.name: Helen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: vernacular form of the name (Greek Hēlēnē) borne in classical legend by a famous beauty, wife of Menelaus, whose seizure by the Trojan prince Paris sparked off the Trojan War. Her name is of uncertain origin; it may be connected with a word meaning ‘ray’ or ‘sunbeam’ compare Greek hēlios ‘sun’. It has sometimes been taken as connected with the Greek word meaning ‘Greek’, Hellēn, but this is doubtful. In the early Christian period the name was borne by the mother of the Emperor Constantine, who is now usually known by the Latin version of her name, Helena. She is credited with having found the True Cross in Jerusalem. She was born in about 248 , probably in Bithynia. However, in medieval England it was believed that she had been born in Britain, which greatly increased the popularity of the name there.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Helen Smythe was born in 1579, in Newport Pagnell, Buckinghamshire, England, her father, Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgate, was 38 and her mother, Anne Lynford, was 38. She had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with John Ryder. She died in 1677, in Northampton, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 98, and was buried in All Saints' Church, Northampton, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Helen Smythe(이)가 1579년 영국 벅잉함실르 뉴포트 파그넬에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgate(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Lynford(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 John Ryder(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1677년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 노르섬프턴에서 98세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 노르섬프턴 앨 세인츠' c후르치에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Helen Smythe родилась в 1579 г. в местности нюпорт пагнелл, буккингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgate было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Lynford, 38. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от John Ryder. Она умерла в 1677 г. в местности нортамптон, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 98 лет и была похоронена в местности алл сеинц' чурч, нортамптон, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.
